topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting personally identifiable information and establishing protocols for data breach notification and security.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to provide criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach involving sensitive personally identifiable information.

Requires a person who owns or possesses data in electronic form containing a means of identification and who has knowledge of a major security breach to provide prompt notice to the U.S. Secret Service or FBI.
Document 2: Instructs the Director of Office of Management and Budget to establish policies for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security involving sensitive personal information.
Requires timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised as a result of a breach.
Document 3: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information.
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as any information contained in a financial record that includes personally identifiable information concerning a consumer.
Document 4: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify an individual within two business days after discovery of any breach of security.
Known to have resulted in the theft or unlawful access of the individual's personally identifiable information.
Document 5: Amends provisions of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act to prohibit the release of student educational records without written parental consent.
Expands the list of individuals exempt from such prohibitions, allowing more access to student records.
Document 6: Defines 'major security breach' as any security breach involving means of identification pertaining to at least 10,000 individuals.
Authorizes the Attorney General to bring civil actions for violations of federal laws relating to data security.

topic.name: Federal Criminal Code Amendments
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various amendments to the federal criminal code aimed at enhancing penalties and enforcement measures related to identity theft, public official accountability, and money laundering.

Document 1: Amends the federal criminal code to provide for a two-year increase in the prison sentence of public officials convicted of any crime.
involving bribery, fraud, extortion, or theft of public funds greater than $10,000.
Document 2: Amends the federal criminal code to eliminate the requirement in a prosecution for identity theft that the prosecution must prove that the defendant knew that the stolen identification documents belonged to another person.
Document 3: directs the Department of Justice (DOJ) to establish procedures to expedite the review of, and seek an indictment in, any case involving identity theft or aggravated identity theft.
DOJ must submit to Congress and publish a report that contains certain information on the status of prosecutions for such offenses.
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to provide secret service protection to former Vice Presidents, their spouses, and their children under 16 years of age.
Identity Theft Enforcement and Restitution Act of 2008 - Amends the federal criminal code to: authorize criminal restitution orders in identity theft cases to compensate victims.
Document 5: amends the federal criminal code to establish enhanced penalties for tax return identity theft crimes and other related fraud.
modifies the state of mind requirement in identity theft prosecutions to eliminate the need to prove that a defendant knew that the means of identification belonged to another person.
Document 6: Amends the federal criminal code to redefine 'specified unlawful activity' for purposes of money laundering provisions as any act constituting an offense in violation of the laws of the United States punishable by imprisonment for a term exceeding one year.
Prohibits: knowingly conducting a monetary transaction involving the proceeds of unlawful activity of a value greater than $10,000.
Document 7: Amends the federal criminal code relating to laundering of monetary instruments to expand the concept of monetary proceeds to include gross receipts.
Directs the U.S. Sentencing Commission to amend its sentencing guidelines to incorporate the amendment made by this Act.

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Reforms
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing the regulation and oversight of financial institutions to ensure stability and transparency in the financial system.

Document 7: Establishes an independent Financial Stability Council to review, and approve or prohibit issuance of, any rule or regulation issued by any federal financial regulator.

topic.description: Legislative changes and proposals related to the management and protection of endangered and threatened species under the Endangered Species Act.

Document 1: This bill amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to prohibit taking endangered or threatened species within the United States or U.S. territorial seas as trophies.
Any person seeking to import a fish or wildlife trophy must submit documentation establishing that the trophy was taken before the enactment of this bill.
Document 2: This bill amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to replace the current standard for awarding court costs, including attorney fees, in citizen suits with the federal judicial code standard for awarding costs to a prevailing party.
Document 4: This bill prohibits the Department of the Interior from using any funds to make a determination on the listing of the northern long-eared bat under the Endangered Species Act of 1973.
Interior proposed in October 2013 to list the northern long-eared bat as endangered.
Document 5: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to prohibit the Secretary of the Interior from adding a species to the list of endangered and threatened species if the Secretary has approved a State Protective Action for such species.
Establishes a process for approving or disapproving a proposed SPA.
Document 6: Amends the Marine Mammal Protection Act of 1972 to allow the importation of polar bear parts taken legally in sport hunts in Canada.
Document 7: Requires an agency when developing or considering approval of a management plan for federal land, to ensure that hunting activities are allowed as a use of such land to the extent that such use is not clearly incompatible with the purposes for which the land is managed.

topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and regulations aimed at improving safety, competition, and infrastructure within the rail transportation system in the United States.

Prohibits the Board from issuing a certificate authorizing construction and operation of railroad lines if it restricts competition.
Document 6: Requires a rail carrier to have a storage plan for any of its rail cars that it stores for three or more years.
Approved by the Surface Transportation Board.
Document 7: Authorizes appropriations for FY2008-FY2011 for the railroad safety program.
Urges the development of new technology to prevent loss of life and injuries at highway-rail grade crossings.

Document 2: Amends the Nuclear Waste Policy Act of 1982 to authorize the Department of Energy to enter into new contracts
Defines 'interim consolidated storage facility' as a facility licensed by the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Energy to carry out nuclear energy research and development
Research related to reactor concepts, small modular reactors, and fuel cycles.
Document 4: Amends the Energy Policy Act of 2005 to add objectives for civilian nuclear energy research
Researching and developing technologies and processes to improve nuclear power systems.
Document 5: Directs the Nuclear Regulatory Commission to establish a procedure to require the designation of spent nuclear fuel
Requires all spent nuclear fuel that is contained in storage pools and qualified to be placed in dry cask storage to be moved.
Document 6: Directs the Secretary of Education to award grants to establish or expand STEM schools
Amends the National Science Foundation Authorization Act of 2002 to extend the Robert Noyce Teacher Scholarship Program.
Document 7: States that it is U.S. policy to support the establishment of an international regime for the assured supply of nuclear fuel
Authorizes the President to make grant basis contributions to the IAEA for an international nuclear fuel bank.

topic.name: Retirement and Employee Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative amendments related to retirement plans, employee benefits, and protections for employees within pension and welfare plans.

Document 4: Amends the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 to authorize the Department of Education to award grants to Indian tribes to develop and maintain programs that support the use of Native American languages as their primary language of instruction.
Document 5: States that nothing in this Act affects the application of the Indian Child Welfare Act of 1978 regarding agreements between states and Indian tribes.
Extends federal recognition to the Chickahominy Tribe.
Document 6: Extends federal recognition to the Little Shell Tribe of Chippewa Indians of Montana.
Makes the Tribe and each member eligible for all services and benefits provided by the United States to Indians.

Have been employed as a full-time teacher for five consecutive complete school years in an Indian school.

Prohibits the availability of such funds for construction, acquisition, or alteration of veterans' medical facilities.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code and Title II of the Social Security Act to exempt from social security taxes remuneration paid to any elected or appointed member of any general governing board.
Remuneration is not more than $1,300 and is paid for service performed on or after January 1, 2008.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Education to make grants to providers of family literacy services to improve the literacy and English skills of limited English proficient individuals.
Directs the Secretary to reserve certain funds to evaluate and improve the family literacy grant program.
